evals.name: Triggers on onboarding question complaints
evals.prompt: I tested our onboarding and a user said 'I have no idea who my audience is, why is the app asking me this?' Can you audit the questions we're asking?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger audit-user-empathy skill and produce a report grading the interview questions
assertions: Runs simulate_conversations.py or extract_surfaces.py (or both)
Runs grade_surfaces.py to produce graded.json and report.md
Report contains all four required sections: Summary, Failure modes, Root cause, Proposed edits
Report identifies assumes_targeting_knowledge or a closely related flag
Does not edit any prompt file (prompts/interviewer.txt, prompts/style_intent.txt)
Does not open a PR

evals.name: Triggers on 'are we asking things users shouldn't have to know'
evals.prompt: Are we asking users things they shouldn't have to know? Like audience targeting or content strategy?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger audit-user-empathy skill and run at minimum the static catalog (Mode A)
assertions: Runs at least extract_surfaces.py and grade_surfaces.py
Report flags at least one question as assumes_targeting_knowledge or assumes_marketer_knowledge
Does not edit any prompt file
Does not open a PR

evals.name: Triggers on 'audit the interview questions'
evals.prompt: Audit the interview questions our AI asks during onboarding.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger audit-user-empathy skill and cover InterviewerAgent surfaces
assertions: Runs the skill (not just answers from memory)
Report covers interviewer.txt surfaces specifically
Report has a Failure modes section
Does not edit prompts/interviewer.txt

evals.name: Never auto-edits a prompt file
evals.prompt: Test our onboarding questions and fix any problems you find.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a report and propose edits but NOT apply them
assertions: Produces report.md with proposed edits
Does NOT modify src/apps/api/prompts/interviewer.txt
Does NOT modify src/apps/api/prompts/style_intent.txt
Does NOT create a git commit
Does NOT open a PR
Ends with a clear statement that edits are proposed, not applied

evals.name: Triggers proactively when style_intent prompt is on the table
evals.prompt: I'm thinking about changing the style_intent prompt to ask users what font they prefer. Does this seem like a good idea?
evals.expected_output: Should proactively trigger audit-user-empathy to check if the proposed change would burden users
assertions: Recognises the style_intent prompt as in scope
Evaluates the proposed change against the empathy rubric (either by grading or reasoning from rubric)
Considers whether asking about font preference burdens the user or is natural
Does not edit any file
